problems.questionText: 다음 문장을 재진술(Paraphrasing)한 것으로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: If good things rest on the motivation of greed, it follows that generous people can never do good to others.
If it is possible that people do good things out of greed, it means that greed may have a bright side in it.
Individuals eager to commit themselves to the good in life can retain the trait until they reach old age.
Even greed may count as a valuable thing in a struggling world, as many of greedy people are men of charity.
problems.passage: If greed motivates individuals to work for the good things in life, there may be a silver lining in the trait.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분과 의미가 가장 가까운 것을 고르시오.
problems.passage: The old man's hair was sparse rather than thick and full like it was when he was young.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분과 의미가 가장 가까운 것을 고르시오.
problems.passage: In 1898 the Spanish ceded, or surrendered possession of, both Guam and Puerto Rico to the United

problems.questionText: 이 단락의 중심 사상은?
choices: 인터넷이 의사소통 방식을 완전히 바꾸어 놓았다.
인터넷이 우리 삶의 여러 영역에 혁신을 가져왔다.
problems.passage: The Internet has revolutionized the way we communicate, work, and access information. Email allows instant global communication, while online platforms enable remote work opportunities. Digital libraries provide access to vast amounts of knowledge. However, this digital revolution also brings challenges such as privacy concerns and information overload.

problems.questionText: 이 단락의 중심 사상은?
choices: 기후변화가 현시대의 가장 시급한 문제 중 하나이다.
기후변화로 인한 구체적인 현상들이 나타나고 있다.
problems.passage: Climate change is one of the most pressing issues of our time. Rising temperatures are melting polar ice caps, leading to sea level rise. Extreme weather events are becoming more frequent and severe. Scientists warn that without immediate action, the consequences could be irreversible.

problems.questionText: 이 단락의 중심 사상은?
choices: 규칙적인 운동이 다양한 건강상의 이익을 제공한다.
problems.passage: Regular exercise provides numerous health benefits. It strengthens the cardiovascular system, reducing the risk of heart disease. Exercise also improves mental health by releasing endorphins, which act as natural mood elevators. Additionally, physical activity helps maintain a healthy weight and builds muscle strength.

problems.questionText: 이 단락의 중심 사상은?
choices: 소셜미디어가 정보 공유와 인간관계 형성 방식을 변화시켰다.
problems.passage: Social media platforms have transformed how people share information and connect with others. Facebook allows users to stay in touch with friends and family across the globe. Twitter enables real-time news sharing and public discourse. Instagram has created new opportunities for artistic expression and business marketing.

problems.questionText: 이 단락의 중심 사상은?
choices: 재생 에너지원이 화석 연료를 대체할 중요한 대안이 되고 있다.
problems.passage: Renewable energy sources are becoming increasingly important as fossil fuel reserves decline. Solar panels convert sunlight into electricity without producing harmful emissions. Wind turbines harness natural wind power to generate clean energy. These sustainable technologies offer hope for a greener future while reducing our dependence on fossil fuels.

problems.questionText: 이 글의 전개 방식은?
choices: 문제 제시 → 원인 분석 → 해결책 제시
현상 설명 → 비교 분석 → 결론 도출
problems.passage: Many students struggle with procrastination during their academic careers. This habit often stems from fear of failure or perfectionism. To overcome procrastination, students can break large tasks into smaller, manageable steps. Additionally, setting specific deadlines and rewarding progress can help maintain motivation.

problems.questionText: 이 글의 전개 방식은?
choices: 과거 상황 → 현재 상황 → 비교 분석
원인 제시 → 결과 설명 → 해결책 제안
problems.passage: In the past, people primarily relied on newspapers and television for news. Today, however, digital platforms dominate the media landscape. Unlike traditional media, online sources provide instant updates and interactive features. This shift has both advantages and disadvantages for information consumption.

problems.questionText: 이 글의 전개 방식은?
problems.passage: Artificial intelligence is rapidly advancing across various industries. For instance, in healthcare, AI helps doctors diagnose diseases more accurately. In transportation, self-driving cars use AI to navigate safely. Similarly, in education, AI-powered systems provide personalized learning experiences for students.

problems.questionText: 빈칸에 들어갈 알맞은 연결어를 고르시오.
problems.passage: The new smartphone has many impressive features. The camera quality is excellent, and the battery life has significantly improved. ________, the price is quite high compared to similar models.

problems.questionText: 빈칸에 들어갈 알맞은 연결어를 고르시오.
problems.passage: Regular reading has numerous cognitive benefits. It improves vocabulary and comprehension skills. ________, reading can reduce stress and provide mental stimulation that helps prevent cognitive decline.

problems.questionText: Many companies are implementing remote work policies. ________, Google allows employees to work from home three days a week, while Microsoft offers completely flexible schedules.

Scientists have been studying the effects of meditation on the brain for decades. Their research shows that regular meditation can improve focus and reduce anxiety. ________, meditation appears to be a valuable tool for mental health.
빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 연결어를 고르시오.
problems.passage: The city's public transportation system has many advantages. It's environmentally friendly and cost-effective. ________, the buses are often overcrowded during rush hours, making the commute uncomfortable.

problems.questionText: 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 어휘를 고르시오.
problems.passage: The rise of fast fashion has had significant environmental consequences. Cheap clothing production leads to massive textile waste and pollution. Many garments are worn only a few times before being discarded. This ________ approach to fashion is unsustainable for our planet.

problems.questionText: 다음 글의 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
problems.passage: Social media platforms collect vast amounts of user data to improve their services. However, this practice raises serious privacy concerns. Users often share personal information without fully understanding how it will be used. Therefore, stronger ________ regulations are needed to protect consumers.

problems.questionText: 다음 글의 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
problems.passage: Traditional farming methods often rely heavily on chemical pesticides and fertilizers. In contrast, organic farming uses natural processes to maintain soil health and control pests. While organic produce may cost more, many consumers are willing to pay the premium for ________ food.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 단어를 고르시오.
problems.passage: The COVID-19 pandemic forced many businesses to adapt quickly to new circumstances. Restaurants began offering delivery and takeout services exclusively. Retail stores developed online shopping platforms. These rapid ______ helped many businesses survive during lockdowns.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 단어를 고르시오.
problems.passage: Sleep deprivation has become increasingly common in modern society. People often sacrifice sleep for work or entertainment. However, lack of sleep can seriously impact physical health, mental performance, and emotional well-being. Therefore, prioritizing adequate sleep should be considered ______ for overall health.

problems.questionText: 다음 중 어법상 어색한 것을 고르시오.
choices: The audience in the stadium were deeply moved at the concert.
The news spreads quickly through social media.
A lot of furniture are for sale now.
At the very moment we were about to leave, it started to rain.
Is there enough room for us?

problems.questionText: 다음 (A), (B), (C)에서 어법에 맞는 표현을 골라 짝지은 것을 고르시오.
problems.passage:
• I will call you twice (A) a / the week.
• They are planning an expedition to climb (B) a / the highest mountain in North America.
• Please hand me (C) a / the pen on the table. I mean the one with the blue stripes.

problems.questionText: 빈칸에 the를 쓸 수 없는 것은?
choices: They rented a car by _______ day.
Investing in new technology is _______ only way to stay ahead.
She is _______ taller than any other girl in the class.
I visited the bookstore on _______ way home.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분의 의미가 잘못된 것은?
choices: You are a man of wisdom. (= wise)
The bus arrived on time today. (= punctually)
They say a new discovery can happen by luck. (= luckily)
It needs to be handled with care. (= carelessly)
She spoke French and Spanish with fluency. (= fluently)

problems.questionText: 다음 문장의 밑줄 친 부분과 바꿔 쓸 수 있는 말은?
problems.passage: My brother was all smiles when he heard the news.

problems.questionText: 다음 문장의 빈칸에 들어갈 알맞은 말은?
problems.passage: The United Nations _______ born in the hope that survived a world war.

problems.questionText: 다음 중 어법상 어색한 문장을 고르시오.
choices: A woman standing behind me on the subway touched me on the shoulder.
I patted Jane through the back.
My sister was holding me by the sleeve while wandering around the amusement park.
I looked him in the eyes.
The ball hit me on the head.

problems.questionText: 다음 중 어법상 어색한 것을 고르시오.
choices: How long does a bar of soap last?
I made two loafs of bread this morning.
She wants to put two spoonfuls of sugar in her coffee.
It can be a useful piece of information.
I bought two pieces of furniture yesterday.

problems.questionText: 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 어색한 것을 고르시오.
choices: She's looking for a position which demands a good command of English.
The souvenir we picked for her was a red scarf.
It was difficult to find a mistake in his history report.
The dog is regarded as a faithful animal.
The children in the playground are all of the age.

problems.questionText: 주어진 문장의 밑줄 친 부분과 쓰임이 같은 것은?
choices: Birds of a feather flock together.
A Mr. Kang came to see you.
Take this medicine three times a day.
You didn't say a word during the meeting.
problems.passage: These gloves are of a size.

problems.questionText: 다음 중 어법상 어색한 것을 고르시오.
choices: He kissed her daughter on the cheek.
The rich are not always happy.
They sell chestnuts by the kilogram.
I have an appointment with the Mr. Kim this evening.
Will you pass me the salt, please?

problems.questionText: 주어진 문장을 설명한 내용으로 적절하지 않은 두 개를 고르시오.
choices: (A)의 on purpose는 ‘purposely’로 바꿔 쓸 수 있다.
(A)의 to avoid traffic은 to부정사의 형용사적 용법으로 쓰였다.
(B)의 of no use는 ‘useless’로 바꾸어 쓸 수 있다.
(B)의 information은 셀 수 있는 명사로 쓰였다.
(C)의 우리말 해석은 ‘저는 선물 포장에 아주 서투릅니다.’이다.
problems.passage:
(A) They chose that particular route on purpose [to avoid traffic].
(B) This outdated information is of no use in our research.
(C) I'm all thumbs when it comes to wrapping gifts.

problems.questionText: 다음의 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 고치시오.
problems.passage:
(A) He usually skips the lunch and takes a nap.
(B) I was listening to music when I suddenly heard a loud crack.
(C) My mom has two pairs of sunglasses.
(D) They went on a three-weeks-long honeymoon.

problems.questionText: 주어진 단어와 어구를 활용하여 다음의 우리말을 영작하시오. (단, 필요시 어형을 바꿀 것)
problems.passage:
젊은 사람들은 위험을 감수하는 것을 두려워해서는 안 된다.
young / afraid of / should / the / not / take risks / be
